questions: Should pinned groups survive when clips are manually deleted by the user? (e.g., user deletes 2 of 5 clips in a pinned group — does the group shrink, or unpin entirely below some threshold?)

For auto-sync (Phase 7): when new images are added to a shot with a pinned group, should the new clips inherit the same duration/timing as existing clips, or should the group expand to accommodate them?
Should 'Switch to Video' be available on suggested groups (auto-pinning first), or only on already-pinned groups?
For shot drops from the shot list: is there an existing drag data format for shots, or does this need a new drag type defined in the shot list/gallery components?
When a pinned group is in video mode and the user manually deletes the video clip, should it auto-switch back to images from snapshot, or just unpin/remove the group?

success_criteria.criterion: All 8 existing useShotGroups tests pass without modification
pinnedShotGroups survives full save/load round-trip: serializeForDisk → validateSerializedConfig → loadTimeline → buildTimelineData
Pinning a suggested group via context menu persists the group in TimelineConfig and it appears with solid border on next render
Unpinning a pinned group removes it from TimelineConfig and it reverts to dashed suggested border (if still inferable)
Switch-to-video snapshots image clips and switch-to-images restores them, each as a single applyEdit call
Undo/redo works for pin, unpin, switch-to-video, and switch-to-images operations
Dragging any clip in a pinned group moves all group clips together using existing multi-drag infrastructure
Inferred groups are suppressed when a pinned group exists for the same shot+track combination
AI timeline agent (supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/) does not break when pinnedShotGroups is present in config
npm run build succeeds with no type errors
Suggested groups render with dashed border; pinned groups render with solid border using existing color-mix pattern (no hardcoded colors)
Pinned groups show shot name label always (not just on hover)
New usePinnedShotGroups hook is under ~150 lines
Shot drop from shot list creates pinned group with all shot images as sequential clips
Auto-sync updates pinned group clips when shot images change via ShotsContext
Manual smoke test: create suggested group → pin → switch to video → undo → switch back to images → unpin → verify dashed border returns
Manual smoke test: drag a pinned group across tracks and verify all clips move together

assumptions: Pinned groups with fewer than 1 clip remaining (after manual deletion) should be auto-removed from config. Groups with 1+ clips survive.
Switch-to-Video is available on suggested groups (auto-pins first, as stated in the idea doc). This keeps the UX simple — users don't need to learn to pin before switching.

The existing drag data format does not include shot-type drags. A new drag type will need to be defined, likely originating from the shot list/gallery components. This is Phase 6 and can be deferred if the shot list doesn't support drag yet.
Auto-sync (Phase 7) will match new image clip duration to the average duration of existing clips in the pinned group.

repairConfig and migrateToFlatTracks in timeline-data.ts/save-utils.ts pass through unknown config keys (they only touch output/clips/tracks). Will verify during implementation and add explicit passthrough if needed.
The AI timeline agent reads TimelineConfig but only uses output/clips/tracks fields. Adding pinnedShotGroups will not break it because it ignores unknown keys. Will verify in Step 5.
imageClipSnapshot stores enough data (clipId, meta, assetKey) to fully restore image clips without re-fetching from the server.
